prompt: We have a compliance policy document (PDF) that references specific software versions, certifications, and regulatory dates. Animate it so it self-checks: when referenced software versions become EOL, when certifications expire, or when regulatory deadlines approach, it should flag the stale sections.

Our team's skills matrix is a static spreadsheet. Make it alive: it should pull from GitHub contributions, completed training courses, and incident response participation to auto-suggest skill level updates, surface training gaps, and identify single points of failure.

We have a static cost allocation spreadsheet that finance uses for cloud billing. Animate it to pull from AWS Cost Explorer daily, allocate costs to teams based on resource tags, flag anomalous spending, and predict monthly totals by day 15 of each month.

Our API specification (OpenAPI YAML) drifts from the actual implementation. Design an animated validation system where the spec auto-tests itself against the running API, marks endpoints that have drifted, and generates pull requests to fix the spec.

We maintain a dependency graph as a static document. Make it self-maintaining: it should detect when new dependencies are added, map their transitive dependency trees, check for known vulnerabilities, and flag license incompatibilities — all triggered by changes to package files.

Our incident postmortem template is a static Google Doc that teams copy. Animate the template itself so it auto-populates timeline data from PagerDuty, pulls relevant logs from the time window, and pre-fills the list of affected services from the alerting system.

We have a dataset of customer support tickets exported as a static JSONL file. Animate it: the file should auto-refresh daily from Zendesk, auto-classify tickets by category using keyword patterns, and publish summary statistics to a dashboard endpoint.

Our SLA dashboard is manually updated from three different data sources every Monday. Make the dashboard self-populating: it should pull uptime from StatusPage, response times from Datadog, and ticket resolution times from Jira, computing SLA compliance in real time.
